notes: IAQ and mould containment training — keep it clinical and professional, NO dramatic or close-up mould and no gore. Technicians in full PPE (respirator, coveralls, gloves, goggles). Real taught methods only — plastic-sheeting containment barriers, negative-pressure HEPA air scrubbers/negative air machines, spore-trap air sampling. Australian settings, metric, 230 V / AU equipment. Bright, clean, well lit.

images.context: Course hero image for 'IAQ and Mould: Understanding Airborne Spread and Containment'
images.prompt: A professional mould remediation technician in full white PPE — respirator, coveralls, gloves and goggles — standing inside a sealed containment zone built from floor-to-ceiling clear plastic sheeting with a zippered entry in an Australian home, a HEPA negative-air machine ducted through the barrier maintaining negative pressure, clean, clinical and well lit. Realistic documentary photography, mid-distance framing.
images.negativePrompt: no on-image text, no logos, no brand names, no watermarks, no gore, no distressing/dramatic mould close-ups, no identifiable face-forward portraits, no visible mould growth, no dirt or grime, no US power outlets

images.context: Module on containment strategies and negative pressure using HEPA air scrubbers
images.prompt: A HEPA air scrubber and negative air machine running inside a sealed remediation containment area with clear plastic sheeting walls, flexible ducting venting through the barrier, a technician in full PPE adjusting the unit, bright clean clinical lighting in an Australian interior. Realistic photography, mid-distance framing on the equipment.

images.context: Module on monitoring and maintaining IAQ after remediation via air sampling
images.prompt: Close mid-distance of a technician's gloved hands operating a calibrated air sampling pump connected to a spore-trap cassette on a small tripod, collecting a post-remediation indoor air sample in a clean, dry Australian room, professional and clinical. Realistic photography, over-shoulder hands-on-task framing.
